channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Visitor finds 2.3-carat diamond at Arkansas state park
item.description: A visitor to the Arkansas Crater of Diamonds State Park left with a 2.3-carat diamond to commemorate the trip.
item.dc:creator: Alex Kienlen, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Texas middle school cheerleaders suffer first and second degree burns after 'punishment' from coach
item.description: Thirteen cheerleaders at Evans Middle School in Lubbock, Texas are suffering from first and second-degree burns on their hands after coach punishes team.
item.dc:creator: Julianna Washburn, KRON4

item.title: Obama’s callout to Black men touches a nerve among Democrats. Is election-year misogyny at play?
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Barack Obama had frank words for Black men who may be considering sitting out the election.
item.dc:creator: MATT BROWN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Heavy Israeli bombardment in northern Gaza as UN peacekeepers in Lebanon are hit again
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Palestinians in northern Gaza described heavy Israeli bombardment Saturday in the hours after airstrikes killed at least 22 people, as Israel continued to tell people there and in southern Lebanon to get out of the way of its offensives against the Hamas and Hezbollah militant groups.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Who is not allowed to vote in the 2024 presidential election?
item.description: The United States is just weeks away from a highly-anticipated presidential election, but not everyone will be able to exercise their right to vote on Nov. 5.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, Fox 5

item.title: Whooping cough cases surge to record levels in US, CDC says
item.description: Whooping cough cases have increased to record levels since 2014, with four times as many cases compared to last year, data from the CDC showed. There have been more than 16,000 cases this year alone and two confirmed deaths, statistics revealed.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Florida deputies help pregnant woman in labor during Milton
item.description: Polk County Sheriff Grady Judd said that some of the office's deputies helped transport a pregnant woman who was going into labor to the hospital early Thursday morning, as Hurricane Milton brought widespread power outages, high winds and flooding to Central Florida.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, WGN 9

item.title: Mindfulness as effective as medication in treating anxiety: Study
item.description: A new study finds that practicing mindfulness can be as effective as taking antidepressants to treat anxiety disorders
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: Key disaster loan program 'quickly exhausting' funds after hurricanes
item.description: Officials are warning a key federal program that provides disaster loans to businesses and homeowners is running critically low on funds in the aftermath of back-to-back hurricanes, increasing the pressure on Congress to return to Washington.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: Kentucky woman accused of dismembering mother’s body, cooking body parts, casting spells
item.description: A Kentucky woman is facing charges after she allegedly dismembered her mother, whose body parts were found on the property, including in the backyard and in the oven.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Aaron Hernandez's 'American Sports Story': Psychologist looks at where NFL superstar's demise may have started
item.description: Forensic psychologist Helen Smith spoke with Fox News Digital about the criminal mind of former New England Patriots tight end Aaron Hernandez and his childhood trauma.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What's behind the northern lights that dazzled the sky farther south than normal
item.description: Another in a series of unusually strong solar storms hitting Earth produced stunning skies full of pinks, purples, greens and blues farther south than normal, including into parts of Germany, the United Kingdom, New England and New York City.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Missing Texas mother: Suzanne Simpson's neighbor witnessed fight
item.description: Simpson, a mother of four and real estate agent, disappeared after an alleged fight with her husband Brad Simpson, 53.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: 3 in 5 millennials want full access to abortion: Study
item.description: The largest percentile of individuals who do not want to live in a "fully legal" abortion state is Gen Z at 33%, followed by Gen X and Baby Boomers (30%).
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, WGN 9

item.title: Residents slog through flooded streets, clear debris after Hurricane Milton tore through Florida
item.description: LITHIA, Fla. (AP) — Florida residents slogged through flooded streets, gathered up scattered debris and assessed damage to their homes on Friday after Hurricane Milton smashed through coastal communities and spawned a barrage of deadly tornadoes.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS O'MEARA, BRENDAN FARRINGTON and TY O'NEIL, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Venice Beach home buried in sand after Milton
item.description: While most people are dealing with water in their homes, some homeowners are battling an inundation of sand.
item.dc:creator: Austin Kellerman, KRON4

item.title: Biden to visit Hurricane Milton-ravaged Florida with damage estimates 'around $50 billion'
item.description: The White House announced President Biden is scheduled to visit Florida on Sunday to survey the aftermath of Hurricane Milton.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trial on hold for New Jersey man charged in knife attack that injured Salman Rushdie
item.description: MAYVILLE, N.Y. (AP) — The attempted murder trial of the man charged with severely injuring author Salman Rushdie in a 2022 knife attack was put on hold Friday while judges consider a request to move it to another county. Jury selection had been scheduled to start on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Colorado gold mine tour guide identified as victim killed in elevator mishap: 'A good man,' sheriff says
item.description: A tour guide for the Mollie Kathleen Gold Mine Tour in Colorado was identified as the lone death following an elevator mishap that trapped 23 others who were rescued.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Florida Gov. DeSantis helps Waffle House employees cook for first responders
item.description: Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis put on the gloves and picked up a spatula at a Sarasota-area Waffle House on Thursday to help cook for first responders after Hurricane Milton left a path of destruction across the Tampa Bay area.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Accettulla, Fox 5

item.title: Fidelity data breach exposed info from 77,000 customers: Was your account compromised?
item.description: Thousands of Fidelity Investments customers may have had their personal information stolen amid a data breach that occurred in August of this year, the financial firm announced.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: Chicago Marathon to take place on Sunday
item.description: The Chicago Marathon will take place on Sunday, October 13, with thousands of runners and spectators expected to attend, and several downtown streets closures already in place.
item.dc:creator: Angelica Sanchez, NewsNation

item.title: Climate change gave significant boost to Milton's destructive rain, winds, scientists say
item.description: Human-caused climate change intensified deadly Hurricane Milton 's rainfall by 20 to 30% and strengthened its winds by about 10%, scientists said in a new flash study.
item.dc:creator: ALEXA ST. JOHN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The Latest: Residents begin repairing damage from Hurricane Milton, which killed at least 10
item.description: Florida residents began repairing damage from Hurricane Milton, which smashed through coastal communities and tore homes to pieces, flooded streets and spawned a deadly tornadoes.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Residents slog through flooded streets, pick up debris after Milton
item.description: Florida residents slogged through flooded streets, gathered up scattered debris and assessed damage to their homes on Friday after Hurricane Milton smashed through coastal communities and tore homes to pieces, flooded streets and spawned a barrage of deadly tornadoes.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Internet Archive data breach exposes more than 31 million user accounts: reports
item.description: The Internet Archive, a popular digital library known for its Wayback Machine, was hacked and suffered a breach that reportedly exposed the data of 31 million user accounts.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, Fox 5

item.title: Biden suggests Trump 'get a life' after false hurricane claims
item.description: “Mr. President Trump, former President Trump, get a life man, help these people,” Biden said in remarks on Hurricane Milton from the White House.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Milton by the numbers: At least 5 dead, at least 12 tornadoes, 3.4M without power
item.description: Hurricane Milton's explosive formation stirred high anxiety for Florida less than two weeks after Helene raked the state and much of the Southeast. On Thursday, authorities were still assessing the damage, even as Gov. Ron DeSantis said it fell short of “the worst-case scenario.
item.dc:creator: MARY KATHERINE WILDEMAN and CHRISTOPHER L. KELLER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: How to help the recovery from Milton, Helene
item.description: Nexstar, the parent company of this website, is teaming up to help support Red Cross efforts to offer critical resources after Milton.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, KRON4

item.title: Police rescue 15 people from Tampa home after Milton knocks tree on house
item.description: Officers with the Tampa Police Department were sheltering a few miles when they were alerted to the incident via a 911 call.
item.dc:creator: Juan Cisneros, KRON4

item.title: Rafael Nadal, 22-time Grand Slam tennis champ, announces retirement
item.description: Rafael Nadal, professional tennis legend and 22-time Grand Slam champion announced his retirement in a video message early Thursday morning.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, Fox 5

item.title: Although Milton has moved on, at least 8 dead and millions in the dark
item.description: Hurricane Milton barreled into the Atlantic Ocean on Thursday after plowing across Florida, where it knocked out power to more than 3 million customers and whipped up a barrage of tornadoes.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Although Milton has moved on, at least 8 dead and millions in the dark
item.description: Hurricane Milton barreled into the Atlantic Ocean on Thursday after plowing across Florida, where it knocked out power to more than 3 million customers and whipped up a barrage of tornadoes.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Video: Wind tears roof off Tropicana Field, home of the Rays
item.description: Hurricane Milton made landfall in Siesta Key south of St. Petersburg, and while the city avoided some of the worst storm surges that doesn't mean the city avoided major effects from the storm.
item.dc:creator: Garrett Phillips, Fox 5

item.title: A New York village known for its majestic mute swans faces a difficult choice after one is killed
item.description: MANLIUS, N.Y. (AP) — Elegant white swans have an outsize presence in this upstate New York village measuring less than 2 square miles. Their likeness is on village flags, community centers and welcome signs. “Swan Fest” is celebrated each fall.
item.dc:creator: CAROLYN THOMPSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Watch: Massive 28-foot wave captured inside Hurricane Milton
item.description: Drone footage captured a monstrous wave inside Hurricane Milton as the powerful storm passed through the Gulf of Mexico on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, Fox 5

item.title: An unusual hurricane season goes from ultra quiet to record busy and spawns Helene and Milton
item.description: Explosively intensifying Hurricane Milton is the latest freaky system to come out of what veteran hurricane scientists call the weirdest storm season of their lives.
item.dc:creator: SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, ABC 4
